title: dryopteris talyschensis
intro: Dryopteris talyschensis, also known as Talysh scaly male fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves, roots, and stems are used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In traditional medicine, Dryopteris talyschensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
